dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8163 IH: Provider Reimbursement Stability Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Murphy (for himself, Mr. Schneider, Mr. Joyce of Pennsylvania, Mr. Suozzi, Mr. Onder, Mr. Panetta, Mrs. Miller-Meeks, Ms. Schrier, and Ms. Kelly of Illinois) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend title XVIII of the Social Security Act to ensure stability for provider payments under the Medicare program.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Provider Reimbursement Stability Act of 2026.
Section 1848(c)(2)(B)(ii)(II) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w–4(c)(2)(B)(ii)(II)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking Subject to and inserting the following:
in item (aa), as inserted by paragraph (1), by striking $20,000,000 and inserting the amount specified in item (bb) for such year; and
by adding at the end the following new items:

item.text: For purposes of item (aa), subject to item (cc), the amount specified in this item is—

subitem.text: for 2028 and each subsequent year, the amount specified in this item for the preceding year.

item.text: For 2032 and every subsequent fifth year, the Secretary shall increase the amount specified in item (bb) for such year by the cumulative percentage increase in the MEI (as defined in section 1842(i)(3)) applicable to physicians’ services for each year occurring during the 5-year period ending on the last day of the preceding year.

section.header: Budget neutrality corrections relating to estimated utilization

subsection.text: Section 1848(c)(2)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w–4(c)(2)(B)) is amended by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.header: Budget neutrality corrections relating to estimated utilization

subclause.text: In the case of a budget neutrality adjustment applied pursuant to clause (ii)(II) for a year (beginning with 2027) that is determined in part using estimated utilization (as defined in subclause (II)(bb)) with respect to a specified service (as defined in subclause (II)(cc)), the Secretary shall, as part of the final rule establishing the physician fee schedule under this section for the assumption correction period (as defined in subclause (II)(aa)) with respect to such year—

item.text: determine the difference between expenditures for such service in such year using estimated utilization and actual utilization for such service (in a manner determined appropriate by the Secretary); and

in the case that the Secretary determines the difference described in item (aa) is greater than the threshold amount (as defined in subclause (II)(dd)) for such year, adjust the conversion factor under this section for such assumption correction period by such amount to reconcile such difference (which may be positive or negative), as determined by the Secretary.
The term assumption correction period means, with respect to a year, the second year beginning after such year.
The term estimated utilization means an estimate of utilization used for purposes of applying clause (ii)(II).
The term specified service means, with respect to a year, a service—

subitem.text: with expected expenditures for such year under this part based on estimated utilization that exceed the threshold amount (as defined in item (dd)) for such year; and
for which payment had been bundled into payment for another service during the preceding year and for which a separate payment or add-on payment is made during such year.

item.text: The term threshold amount means, with respect to a year, 0.1 percent of the total estimated expenditures under this part for services furnished under this section during such year.

subsection.header: Nonapplication of budget neutrality to reconciliation adjustments
subsection.text: Section 1848(c)(2)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w–4(c)(2)(B)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subclause (V), by striking and at the end;
in subclause (VI), by striking the period and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following new subclause:

subclause.text: clause (vii)(I)(bb) for an assumption correction period (as defined in clause (vii)(II)) shall not be taken into account in applying clause (ii)(II) with respect to such period.

paragraph.text: in clause (v), by adding at the end the following new subclause:

subclause.header: Reductions attributable to an assumption correction
subclause.text: For an assumption correction period (as defined in clause (vii)(II)), reduced expenditures attributable to application of clause (vii)(I)(bb) with respect to such period.

section.header: Timely updates to direct costs used to calculate practice expense RVUs
section.text: Section 1848(c)(2)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w–4(c)(2)(B)), as amended by section 3, is further amended by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.header: Timely updates to direct costs used to calculate practice expense relative value units

subclause.header: Simultaneous updates to direct cost inputs at least once every 5 years
subclause.text: The Secretary shall, not less often than every 5 years, update the prices and rates, as applicable, on a category-wide basis for each of the categories of direct cost inputs described in subclause (II) used in the methodology for calculating the practice expense relative value units under this subsection for physicians’ services. Updates made pursuant to the previous sentence shall be made in the same year for all categories of direct cost inputs described in such subclause.

For purposes of this clause, the categories of direct cost inputs described in this subclause are clinical staff wage rates, prices of medical supplies, prices of equipment, and any other category of such inputs used in the methodology described in subclause (I) (as specified by the Secretary).
In making the updates under this clause, the Secretary shall consult with relevant stakeholders, including physician specialty societies.

section.header: Limitation on year-to-year conversion factor variance
section.text: Section 1848(c)(2)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1395w–4(c)(2)(B)), as amended by sections 3 and 4, is further amended by adding at the end the following new clause:

subclause.text: Beginning with 2027, the Secretary may not, for purposes of complying with clause (ii)(II), apply a budget neutrality adjustment to a conversion factor established under subsection (d) for such year that would cause such factor, not taking into account any adjustment to such factor for such year provided under such subsection, to vary by more than 2.5 percent compared to such factor so established for the preceding year.

subclause.header: Continued applicability of budget neutrality requirement
subclause.text: Nothing in subclause (I) may be construed to alter the requirement described in clause (ii)(II).
